---
title: "Compute Climate Normals"
engine: jupyter      
jupyter: myproj  
---

Aggregate temperature and precipitation data for each climate period by longitude, latitude and month number producing a single value per grid cell and month number.

### ***Climatological Reference Period***
```{python}
#| label: crp
crp_beg = 1961
crp_end = 1990
```

### ***Recent Climate Period***
```{python}
#| label: rcp
rcp_beg = 2011
rcp_end = 2020
```

### ***Packages***
```{python}
#| label: packages
import xarray as xr
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import os
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
```

### ***Directories***
```{python}
#| label: paths
dir_rd = "_data/01-era5-download/"
dir_wr = "_data/02-climate-normals/"
```

### ***Functions***

```{python }
#| label: functions
def fn_climate_normals(
    dir_rd: str,
    dir_wr: str,
    cp: str,
    cv: str,
    vnm: str):

    fil = cp + "_" + cv + "_monthly.nc"
    pth = Path(dir_rd) / fil
    ds0 = xr.open_dataset(
        pth,
        engine="h5netcdf",
        chunks="auto", 
        decode_cf=True
    )

    # Select deterministic member if an ensemble dim exists
    if "number" in ds0.dims:
        # If 0 exists, pick it; else pick the first available
        sel_val = 0 if 0 in ds0["number"].values else ds0["number"].values[0]
        ds0 = ds0.sel(number=sel_val)

    # If the coordinate is named valid_time, rename it to time
    if "valid_time" in ds0.coords and "time" not in ds0.coords:
        ds0 = ds0.rename({"valid_time": "time"})
            
    # add month coordinate
    if "month" not in ds0.coords:
        ds0 = ds0.assign_coords(mth=ds0["time"].dt.month)

    # --- build climatology ---
    clim_mon = ds0[vnm].groupby("mth").mean("time", keep_attrs=True)

    # --- convert to a tidy DataFrame ---
    # This will create columns: [lon, lat, mth, var1, var2, ...]
    df = clim_mon.to_dataframe().reset_index()

    # optional: rename coords for consistency
    df = df.rename(columns={"longitude": "lon", "latitude": "lat"})

    # drop unused ensemble number
    df_new = df.drop(columns=["number"])

    # downcast columns to reduce size
    df_new = df_new.astype({"lon": "float32", "lat": "float32"})
    df_new = df_new.astype({"mth": "int8"})
    #df_new ["small_ints"] = pd.to_numeric(df["small_ints"], downcast="integer")

    # --- save as parquet ---
    fil = cp + "_" + cv + "_normals.parquet"
    pth = Path(dir_wr) / fil
    df_new.to_parquet(pth, index=False)

```

### ***Calculate Climate Normals***

```{python}
#| label: main
start = datetime.now()
fn_climate_normals(dir_rd, dir_wr, "crp", "t", "t2m")
fn_climate_normals(dir_rd, dir_wr, "crp", "p", "tp")
fn_climate_normals(dir_rd, dir_wr, "rcp", "t", "t2m")
fn_climate_normals(dir_rd, dir_wr, "rcp", "p", "tp")
end = datetime.now()
print("elapsed time: " + str(end - start))
```

### ***Check results***

```{python}
#| label: check
cp = "crp"
cv = "t"
fil = cp + "_" + cv + "_normals.parquet"
pth = Path(dir_wr) / fil
df = pd.read_parquet(pth)
df.info()
df

mth_counts = df['mth'].value_counts()
mth_counts
```
